Package com.audiodo.aspen

Class ICalibrationCapability

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.audiodo.aspen.ICalibrationCapability
  • public class ICalibrationCapability
extends java.lang.Object
    The CalibrationCapability

    This class is an interface to the calibration capability available on a IDeviceService.
    Through it a user may run a calibration and get a calibration result.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor Description
      protected ICalibrationCapability​(long cPtr, boolean cMemoryOwn)  

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      void delete()  
      protected void finalize()  
      protected static long getCPtr​(ICalibrationCapability obj)  
      UintVector getFrequencies()
      Get a vector with the frequencies used in the calibration.
      long getProgress()
      Get calibration progress
      CalibrationCapabilityProgressData getProgressData()
      Get detailed calibration progress data

      The function can be used to get more detailed information regarding the
      progress of a calibration.
      ICalibrationResult getResult()
      Returns the calibration result

      Returns a result if a calibration has been completed.
      CalibrationCapabilityStatus getStatus()
      Get the current status of the calibration
      void pause()
      Pause the ongoing calibration

      Pause the ongoing calibration.
      boolean restartFrequency()
      Restart frequency test

      This function allows a user to restart the test of a frequency.
      It is only possible to restart on a frequency, not on a specific tone.
      By tone is meant a frequency with an amplitude.
      Which frequency that is restarted depends on the current progress:
      - If playing the second tone or more, the current frequency is restarted
      - If playing the first tone of a frequency, the previous frequency is restarted
      - If playing the first tone of a frequency on the second ear, the last frequency
      on the previous ear is restarted

      It is not possible to restart the frequency when playing the first tone on the first
      frequency on first ear.
      The user can restart the previous frequency until the first frequency on first ear.
      boolean resume()
      Resume an interrupted calibration

      Resumes an interrupted calibration if everything is OK again.
      boolean start​(ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks cbs)
      Starts a new calibration

      Any calibration previously in progress will be overwritten and a new one
      will commence.
      void stop()
      Stops a calibration

      Stops an ongoing calibration.
      protected void swigSetCMemOwn​(boolean own)  
      void userPressedNo()
      Called when a user pressed no

      Updates the calibration runner with the information that the user pressed no.
      "tone_not_playing"-callback will be called when the tone has stopped playing.
      void userPressedYes()
      Called when a user pressed yes

      Updates the calibration runner with the information that the user pressed yes.
      "tone_not_playing"-callback will be called when the tone has stopped playing.

    • Method Detail

      • getCPtr

        protected static long getCPtr​(ICalibrationCapability obj)

      • finalize

        protected void finalize()
        Overrides:
        finalize in class java.lang.Object

      • delete

        public void delete()

      • start

        public boolean start​(ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks cbs)
        Starts a new calibration

        Any calibration previously in progress will be overwritten and a new one
        will commence. The first tone will start playing as soon as possible and
        will be signaled by a "tone_playing" callback.

        Parameters:
        cbs - A pointer to an implemented ICalibrationCapabilityCallbacks class

        Returns:
        bool Returns true if calibration was successfully started, false if not.

      • stop

        public void stop()
        Stops a calibration

        Stops an ongoing calibration. This should be called in all circumstances when
        leaving the calibration for any reason except a completed calibration. Else
        tone generation will commence in the audio device. If a calibration is not in
        progress a call to this method will be ignored.

      • userPressedYes

        public void userPressedYes()
        Called when a user pressed yes

        Updates the calibration runner with the information that the user pressed yes.
        "tone_not_playing"-callback will be called when the tone has stopped playing.

      • userPressedNo

        public void userPressedNo()
        Called when a user pressed no

        Updates the calibration runner with the information that the user pressed no.
        "tone_not_playing"-callback will be called when the tone has stopped playing.

      • getProgress

        public long getProgress()
        Get calibration progress

        Returns:
        A value representing the progress in percent

      • getProgressData

        @Nullable
public CalibrationCapabilityProgressData getProgressData()
        Get detailed calibration progress data

        The function can be used to get more detailed information regarding the
        progress of a calibration. The calibration is performed by playing
        a certain number of intensities for each frequency. For each channel
        a certain number of frequencies are played. E.g. five intensities across
        six frequencies. This is repeated for two channels.

        Returns:
        A CalibrationCapabilityProgressData object containing detailed
        data of the progress or NULL if there is no calibration in progress or
        no calibration has been completed.

      • getResult

        @Nullable
public ICalibrationResult getResult()
        Returns the calibration result

        Returns a result if a calibration has been completed. If not it will return nullptr.
        Result will be kept until a new calibration is started.

        Returns:
        A pointer to the calibration result, or nullptr if calibration is not complete

      • resume

        public boolean resume()
        Resume an interrupted calibration

        Resumes an interrupted calibration if everything is OK again. If
        the calibration has been paused for more than 60 seconds the
        calibration will be stopped and the aborted callback will be
        called.

        Returns:
        True if calibration was able to be resumed, false if it was
        not able to be resumed.

      • getStatus

        public CalibrationCapabilityStatus getStatus()
        Get the current status of the calibration

        Returns:
        The current status of the calibration capability.

      • pause

        public void pause()
        Pause the ongoing calibration

        Pause the ongoing calibration. On resume the calibration will
        restart from the beginning of the current frequency. The
        calibration is only allowed to be paused for up to 60 seconds. If
        its paused any longer the resume call will fail.

      • getFrequencies

        public UintVector getFrequencies()
        Get a vector with the frequencies used in the calibration.

        Returns:
        std::vector<uint16_t> Returns a vector with the frequencies used in the calibration.

      • restartFrequency

        public boolean restartFrequency()
        Restart frequency test

        This function allows a user to restart the test of a frequency.
        It is only possible to restart on a frequency, not on a specific tone.
        By tone is meant a frequency with an amplitude.
        Which frequency that is restarted depends on the current progress:
        - If playing the second tone or more, the current frequency is restarted
        - If playing the first tone of a frequency, the previous frequency is restarted
        - If playing the first tone of a frequency on the second ear, the last frequency
        on the previous ear is restarted

        It is not possible to restart the frequency when playing the first tone on the first
        frequency on first ear.
        The user can restart the previous frequency until the first frequency on first ear.

        Returns:
        True if successful, false otherwise
